activities_projects.title: Poem Reading Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the basic idea of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main message of the poem
Write 3–4 sentences summarizing what the poet wants us to remember
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the central idea and theme

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the theme of unity and equality
steps: Find lines that explain why no men are foreign
Write how the poet compares people from different countries
Explain in simple words
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the theme of universal brotherhood

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words and improve comprehension
steps: Find words like uniforms, dispossess, outrage, innocence
Write their meanings
Use each word in a simple sentence

activities_projects.title: Identify Key Lines
activities_projects.objective: Recognize important lines conveying the poem’s message
steps: Underline or write 5 lines that emphasize human unity
Explain why these lines are important
Share your explanation in class

activities_projects.title: Feelings and Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Understand emotions expressed in the poem
steps: Write how the poet feels about war and hatred
Write why he wants people to remember human unity
Explain in 2–3 sentences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of poet’s feelings and moral

activities_projects.title: Drawing Activity
steps: Draw people of different countries holding hands or helping each other
Label the drawing to show unity and peace
Share your illustration in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages creativity and visualization of the theme

activities_projects.title: Reading Aloud Activity
steps: Practice reading a stanza aloud
Focus on correct pronunciation and pauses
Read it aloud in class

activities_projects.title: Discussion Activity
steps: Discuss why people should not hate others because of nationality
Write 2–3 points from your discussion
Explain in simple words why unity is important

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to personal life
steps: Write 5–6 sentences about how you can promote peace and unity among friends or classmates
Include simple actions or examples

activities_projects.title: Moral Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Understand and explain the message of the poem
steps: Write the moral of the poem in your own words
Explain how remembering that no men are foreign can make the world peaceful
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops moral understanding and personal reflection
